evals.prompt: I have a screen recording of our heart refill popup animation at /tmp/refill_popup.mov and the UI detection JSON at /tmp/refill_popup_detect.json. Can you analyse the animation and extract keyframe data from the video? The popup shows a button tap, three hearts filling up sequentially, and then the popup dismissing with an overlay fade.
evals.expected_output: A valid schema 6.1.0 JSON file with animated_objects covering all visible UI elements (button, hearts, overlay), correct easing assignments, proper track-to-object matching with detailed source_evidence, and both source_category and object_category fields. All detection JSON objects should be accounted for in animated_objects, inferred_objects, or not_in_clip.

assertions.text: Output JSON has schema field set to '6.1.0'
Every detection JSON object appears in animated_objects, inferred_objects, or not_in_clip
Each animated_object has both source_category (raw) and object_category (enum) fields
All easing values in keyframes are from the allowed set: linear, ease_in, ease_out, ease_in_out, spring, constant
Every target object has a path_validated boolean field

evals.prompt: I recorded a UI animation at /tmp/level_complete.mov and have the detection JSON at /tmp/level_complete_detect.json. The CV pipeline found 0 tracks -- the animation is mostly fades and sprite changes that OpenCV can't detect. Can you still extract the animation data?
evals.expected_output: A valid schema 6.1.0 JSON with meta.phase1_quality set to 'no_tracks'. All objects should be inferred from visual evidence (first/last frame comparison). Confidence scores should be moderate (max 0.70). All presence_status values should be inferred_static or inferred_attached. No hallucinated track_ids.

assertions.text: No animated_object has non-empty track_ids (since there are 0 CV tracks)
All presence_status values are inferred_static or inferred_attached (not tracked)
No match_confidence exceeds 0.70 (since all objects are inferred)

evals.prompt: ok so I've got this recording of our game's tile matching animation -- it's at /tmp/tile_match.mov and the detection is at /tmp/tile_match_detect.json. But the CV pipeline went crazy and found like 57 tracks. Most of them look like noise. Can you clean this up and figure out which tracks are real?
evals.expected_output: Agent should either rerun the CV pipeline with higher parameters or proceed with aggressive filtering. Output JSON should have significantly fewer animated_objects than 57. Noise tracks should be listed in unmatched_tracks with reasons. The agent should apply jitter filtering (Rule 7) and motion validation (Rule 13) to separate real animations from noise.

assertions.text: Number of animated_objects is significantly less than 57 (the number of raw tracks)
track_matching.unmatched_tracks contains entries for noise tracks with reasons
Agent either reran CV with higher parameters or applied aggressive filtering (mentioned in reasoning)
No false translation keyframes from sub-5px movements
